content:
# The Shawshank Redemption (1994)

**Genres**: Drama, Crime
**Director**: Frank Darabont
**Cast**: Tim Robbins, Morgan Freeman, Bob Gunton, Clancy Brown, Mark Rolston

## Overview
Framed in the 1940s for the double murder of his wife and her lover, upstanding banker Andy Dufresne begins a new life at the Shawshank prison, where he puts his accounting skills to work for an amoral warden. During his long stretch in prison, Dufresne comes to be admired by the other inmates -- including an older prisoner named Red -- for his integrity and unquenchable sense of hope.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 1994
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.5/10
- Runtime: 142.0 minutes

# The Godfather (1972)

**Genres**: Drama, Crime
**Director**: Francis Ford Coppola
**Cast**: Marlon Brando, Al Pacino, James Caan, Richard S. Castellano, Robert Duvall

## Overview
Spanning the years 1945 to 1955, a chronicle of the fictional Italian-American Corleone crime family. When organized crime family patriarch, Vito Corleone barely survives an attempt on his life, his youngest son, Michael steps in to take care of the would-be killers, launching a campaign of bloody revenge.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 1972
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.4/10
- Runtime: 175.0 minutes

# Fight Club (1999)

**Genres**: Drama
**Director**: David Fincher
**Cast**: Edward Norton, Brad Pitt, Meat Loaf, Jared Leto, Helena Bonham Carter

## Overview
A ticking-time-bomb insomniac and a slippery soap salesman channel primal male aggression into a shocking new form of therapy. Their concept catches on, with underground "fight clubs" forming in every town, until an eccentric gets in the way and ignites an out-of-control spiral toward oblivion.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 1999
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.3/10
- Runtime: 139.0 minutes

# Schindler's List (1993)

**Genres**: Drama, History, War
**Director**: Steven Spielberg
**Cast**: Liam Neeson, Ben Kingsley, Ralph Fiennes, Caroline Goodall, Jonathan Sagall

## Overview
The true story of how businessman Oskar Schindler saved over a thousand Jewish lives from the Nazis while they worked as slaves in his factory during World War II.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 1993
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.3/10
- Runtime: 195.0 minutes

# Spirited Away (2001)

**Genres**: Fantasy, Adventure, Animation, Family
**Director**: Hayao Miyazaki
**Cast**: Rumi Hiiragi, Miyu Irino, Mari Natsuki, Takashi Naito, Yasuko Sawaguchi

## Overview
A ten year old girl who wanders away from her parents along a path that leads to a world ruled by strange and unusual monster-like animals. Her parents have been changed into pigs along with others inside a bathhouse full of these creatures. Will she ever see the world how it once was?

## Key Information
- Release Year: 2001
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.3/10
- Runtime: 125.0 minutes

# The Godfather: Part II (1974)

**Genres**: Drama, Crime
**Director**: Francis Ford Coppola
**Cast**: Al Pacino, Robert Duvall, Diane Keaton, Robert De Niro, John Cazale

## Overview
In the continuing saga of the Corleone crime family, a young Vito Corleone grows up in Sicily and in 1910s New York. In the 1950s, Michael Corleone attempts to expand the family business into Las Vegas, Hollywood and Cuba.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 1974
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.3/10
- Runtime: 200.0 minutes

# Pulp Fiction (1994)

**Genres**: Thriller, Crime
**Director**: Quentin Tarantino
**Cast**: John Travolta, Samuel L. Jackson, Uma Thurman, Bruce Willis, Ving Rhames

## Overview
A burger-loving hit man, his philosophical partner, a drug-addled gangster's moll and a washed-up boxer converge in this sprawling, comedic crime caper. Their adventures unfurl in three stories that ingeniously trip back and forth in time.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 1994
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.3/10
- Runtime: 154.0 minutes

# Whiplash (2014)

**Genres**: Drama
**Director**: Damien Chazelle
**Cast**: Miles Teller, J.K. Simmons, Melissa Benoist, Austin Stowell, Jayson Blair

## Overview
Under the direction of a ruthless instructor, a talented young drummer begins to pursue perfection at any cost, even his humanity.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 2014
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.3/10
- Runtime: 105.0 minutes

# The Dark Knight (2008)

**Genres**: Drama, Action, Crime, Thriller
**Director**: Christopher Nolan
**Cast**: Christian Bale, Heath Ledger, Aaron Eckhart, Michael Caine, Maggie Gyllenhaal

## Overview
Batman raises the stakes in his war on crime. With the help of Lt. Jim Gordon and District Attorney Harvey Dent, Batman sets out to dismantle the remaining criminal organizations that plague the streets. The partnership proves to be effective, but they soon find themselves prey to a reign of chaos unleashed by a rising criminal mastermind known to the terrified citizens of Gotham as the Joker.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 2008
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.2/10
- Runtime: 152.0 minutes

# The Green Mile (1999)

**Genres**: Fantasy, Drama, Crime
**Director**: Frank Darabont
**Cast**: Tom Hanks, Michael Clarke Duncan, David Morse, Bonnie Hunt, James Cromwell

## Overview
A supernatural tale set on death row in a Southern prison, where gentle giant John Coffey possesses the mysterious power to heal people's ailments. When the cellblock's head guard, Paul Edgecomb, recognizes Coffey's miraculous gift, he tries desperately to help stave off the condemned man's execution.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 1999
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.2/10
- Runtime: 189.0 minutes

# Forrest Gump (1994)

**Genres**: Comedy, Drama, Romance
**Director**: Robert Zemeckis
**Cast**: Tom Hanks, Robin Wright, Gary Sinise, Mykelti Williamson, Sally Field

## Overview
A man with a low IQ has accomplished great things in his life and been present during significant historic events - in each case, far exceeding what anyone imagined he could do. Yet, despite all the things he has attained, his one true love eludes him. 'Forrest Gump' is the story of a man who rose above his challenges, and who proved that determination, courage, and love are more important than ability.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 1994
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.2/10
- Runtime: 142.0 minutes

# Once Upon a Time in America (1984)

**Genres**: Drama, Crime
**Director**: Sergio Leone
**Cast**: Robert De Niro, James Woods, Elizabeth McGovern, Joe Pesci, Burt Young

## Overview
A former Prohibition-era Jewish gangster returns to the Lower East Side of Manhattan over thirty years later, where he once again must confront the ghosts and regrets of his old life.

## Key Information
- Release Year: 1984
- Type: Movie
- Rating: 8.2/10
- Runtime: 229.0 minutes

metadata.title: Once Upon a Time in America
